title: Make every alert actionable
description: Page only on symptoms a human must act on, and route everything else to dashboards or tickets.
body: Use when deciding what should page versus merely record. Do not alert on every threshold crossing — non-actionable pages cause fatigue, and a fatigued on-call misses the real one. Actions: alert on user-facing symptoms (error rate, latency, budget burn) rather than causes, attach a runbook link to each alert, and demote informational conditions to dashboards or tickets. Validation signals include every alert mapping to a documented action and a low false-page rate. Risk boundary: silencing or deleting an alert can hide a real outage — record why and prefer routing over deletion, escalating changes to paging policy.
